name: Australia
contextBlurb: Australia is moving to formalise stablecoin oversight. In September 2025, ASIC granted class relief so intermediaries can distribute a named AUD-backed stablecoin issued by an AFS-licensed issuer, subject to conditions such as making the PDS available to retail clients; the relief is time-limited and linked to ASIC Instrument 2025/631. In October 2025, the Government released exposure-draft payments licensing reforms proposing a graduated framework for stored-value facilities that would capture stablecoin issuers/wallets holding customer funds. Until those reforms are enacted, issuers may require an AFSL where the coin constitutes a financial product (e.g., a non-cash payment facility), and intermediaries can rely on the targeted ASIC relief.

legislation.title: ASIC opens consultation to extend the stablecoin distribution exemption.
ASIC grants class relief for intermediaries distributing a Named AUD-backed stablecoin issued by an AFS-licensed issuer

stablecoinMeta.summaryEn: Australia is moving to formalise stablecoin oversight. In September 2025, ASIC granted class relief so intermediaries can distribute a named AUD-backed stablecoin issued by an AFS-licensed issuer, subject to conditions such as making the PDS available to retail clients; the relief is time-limited and linked to ASIC Instrument 2025/631. In October 2025, the Government released exposure-draft payments licensing reforms proposing a graduated framework for stored-value facilities that would capture stablecoin issuers/wallets holding customer funds. Until those reforms are enacted, issuers may require an AFSL where the coin constitutes a financial product (e.g., a non-cash payment facility), and intermediaries can rely on the targeted ASIC relief.
stablecoinMeta.classificationNote: Stablecoins are classified by function under the Corporations Act. Where a token enables payments, it will often be a non-cash payment (NCP) facility, making it a financial product that triggers AFSL and disclosure obligations. regulators.name: Australian Securities and Investments Commission
regulators.role: ASIC is the primary regulator for stablecoins, overseeing licensing of issuers, granting temporary exemptions for distributors, and enforcing compliance with financial services laws.

regulators.name: Treasury
regulators.role: Treasury is responsible for developing and proposing legislation to regulate digital asset platforms, stablecoins, and payment services, including the exposure draft legislation released in 2025.
